races.race_name: Gieves And Hawkes Supports The YCC Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Alma Latina
runners.spotlight: Out of a 1m2f Listed winner for her connections; has duly shown clear promise in two fillies' novice events over this trip, close second at Salisbury (good) latest; still open to progress and warrants respect.
runners.comment: Clear promise in a couple of 1m2f events, close second at Salisbury latest; respected

runners.name: Fozzie
runners.spotlight: Has shown improvement with a couple of second-place finishes this term since being gelded, latest at Redcar (1m, good); one of the form contenders but pedigree raises a stamina doubt upped further in trip.
runners.comment: One of the main form contenders but pedigree raises a stamina doubt over new trip

runners.name: According To Mark
runners.spotlight: Eye-catcher in sole 2yo start; went very close at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) on reappearance and the winner boosted that form by seeming to run well in the Chester Vase since; strong contender.
runners.comment: Went close at Redcar on reappearance and the winner has boosted the form since

runners.name: Maid On The Shelf
runners.spotlight: Brings easily the worst form, having been comfortably held in 8.5f AW contest at Wolverhampton ten weeks ago; best watched unless the market suggests she's likely to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Comfortably held in 8.5f AW contest at Wolverhampton

races.race_name: HBC Construction Ltd Supports The YCC EBF Maiden Stakes (For Horses Which Have Never Run) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Avionics
runners.spotlight: 45,000euros yearling, 150,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; Kodi Bear half-brother to German winners Willy Campbell (7f-1m1f) and Jane Garfield (6f), out of a maiden (RPR 69); the biggest eye-catcher in terms of sales info; respected.
runners.comment: 150,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; the biggest eye-catcher in terms of sales info; respected

runners.name: Kochella
runners.spotlight: 48,000euros foal, 71,000euros yearling, 72,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; Kodiac half-sister to 6f/7f winner Nights Over Egypt (including AW; RPR 79); dam unplaced 7f/1m AW 2yo (56); others have stronger-looking claims.
runners.comment: 72,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; by Kodiac; others have stronger-looking claims

runners.name: Desert Phantom
runners.spotlight: 42,000gns foal, 45,000gns yearling, 55,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; by Blue Point out of 7f AW 2yo winner (RPR 71); less interesting than some of the others in this field.
runners.comment: 55,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; by Blue Point; less interesting than some of these rivals

runners.name: Raslan
runners.spotlight: Attractively bred colt, by New Bay and first foal of a 5f/6f winner (including debut and 2yo Group 3; RPR 110) for his connections; interesting contender, especially if the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: First foal of a sprint winner (including debut and 2yo Group 3) for his connections

runners.name: Boleto
runners.spotlight: 90,000euros foal, £105,000 yearling; by Wootton Bassett out of maiden (RPR 64) half-sister to Group 1 winners Broome (1m4f) and Diego Velazquez (1m) and daughter of of 5f 2yo Listed scorer; looks one of the likelier types.
runners.comment: £105,000 yearling; by Wootton Bassett; grandson of a 2yo Listed scorer; likely type

runners.name: Inns And Out
runners.spotlight: 1,500euros foal, 6,000euros yearling; by Inns Of Court out of maiden (RPR 45) half-sister to German 1,000 Guineas winner Electrelane; the least appealing contender.
runners.comment: 6,000euros yearling; by Inns Of Court; the least appealing contender

runners.name: Shemiyla Star
runners.spotlight: 62,000gns yearling; by Blue Point; sibling to seven winners, notably Sheraz (1m3f-1m7f including Listed; 116) and Lakota Sioux (7f 2yo including Group 3; 100); dam 1m2f scorer (including Group 3; 109); nicely bred.
runners.comment: 62,000gns yearling; by Blue Point; sibling to seven winners; nicely bred

races.race_name: GV Co Property Consultants Supports The YCC Handicap

runners.name: Bravo Zulu
runners.spotlight: Multiple 7f-8.5f winner who fared better than seemed likely when dropped to 6f at Redcar (good to firm; half-length second) last week; fighting chance off same mark.
runners.comment: Half-length second when dropped to 6f at Redcar last week; fighting chance

runners.name: Dashing Dick
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a success but has contrastingly started this campaign in poor form; hasn't won away from Newmarket since 2022; others preferred.
runners.comment: Has a big doubt over current form and is ideally suited by Newmarket

runners.name: Mr Cool
runners.spotlight: Campaigned over 7f of late, generally leaving the impression he needs a return to 6f; has gained both wins at this distance and he's very attractively treated off current mark; respected.
runners.comment: Campaigned over 7f of late; return to 6f looks ideal and he's on a very attractive mark

runners.name: Maris Angel
runners.spotlight: Absent since a respectable effort on Boxing Day when bidding for a Wolverhampton AW five-timer; only 1-9 on turf and is 12lb higher than last attempt in this sphere.
runners.comment: Good spell on AW leaves her 12lb higher than last turf attempt; back from layoff

runners.name: The Good Biscuit
runners.spotlight: Below par last time but consistent otherwise of late and may well rebound, especially back at Pontefract; form here features two wins in 2025 and a close second this spring.
runners.comment: Form at Pontefract features two wins in 2025 and a close second this spring

runners.name: Irish Nectar
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-6, mostly on AW, for current yard; ran encouragingly last time but chance back on turf may depend on the weather's actions; has done his winning on soft ground.
runners.comment: Chance may depend on the weather's actions; all wins on soft ground

runners.name: Alpha Capture
runners.spotlight: Form for new yard this term comprises two placed efforts over 1m and a running-on sixth over 7f; useful over 6f early in career but isn't crying out for the return to this trip.
runners.comment: Useful over 6f early in career but isn't crying out for the return to this trip

runners.name: Aberama Gold
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner down the years and gained his most recent success (narrowly, three starts ago) off current mark; subsequent dip in form is the negative.
runners.comment: Gained most recent success (narrowly, three starts ago) off current mark

runners.name: Rock Opera
runners.spotlight: In reasonably good form having made the frame in last two starts, rallying both times; on a two-year losing spell but remain 7lb below last winning mark; enters calculations.
runners.comment: On a two-year losing spell but is in form and remains 7lb below last winning mark

runners.name: Emperor Caradoc
runners.spotlight: Still on career-high mark but latest effort (when bidding for 6f double) suggests he remains in form, and his Pontefract record is largely consistent; solid contender.
runners.comment: Remains in form; Pontefract record is largely consistent; solid contender

races.race_name: Lycetts Supports The YCC Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Zarinca
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who most recently prevailed in Southwell maiden (6f; kept to AW) on seasonal debut; pedigree suggests she should do better still and prove as effective on turf; respected handicap debutante.
runners.comment: Prevailed in Southwell maiden on seasonal debut; likely to do better still; respected

runners.name: Egotistical
runners.spotlight: Newmarket raider who looks open to further progress; has made a solid start to her career, with three placed efforts preceding a Redcar novice success (6f, good to firm); one of two handicap debutantes who figure highly in calculations.
runners.comment: Solid start to her career; may well build on Redcar novice win and improve further

runners.name: Saucy Jane
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-12; well treated on best efforts but is starting to go the wrong way and ran to nowhere near her rating in 5f seller most recently; chance largely depends on the effects of first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Starting to go the wrong way; needs help from first-time cheekpieces
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 16 And Jmr Partners

runners.name: Storm Esme
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-3 last year suggested she could prove a progressive type this term; that hasn't hitherto materialised but her two spring defeats came in higher grades; may be capable of a bigger effort at this level.
runners.comment: Spring defeats came in higher grades; may post a bigger effort at this level

runners.name: Ruby Red Gove
runners.spotlight: AW filly whose record is 1-6, the success at Wolverhampton on handicap debut; form dipped last time when switched to Polytrack from Tapeta; guesswork is involved as regards another change of surface.
runners.comment: Guesswork is involved as regards another change of surface
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services & Alan Gove Racing

runners.name: Rogue Temptation
runners.spotlight: Registered a novice success last July and good effort on handicap debut in September but not in the same form since; hopes of a resurgence are mostly pinned on the drop in grade off a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Hopes of resurgence are mostly pinned on the drop in grade

runners.name: Diamont Katie
runners.spotlight: Back in good form since having wind surgery and returned to turf from AW, winning comfortably over C&D (good) then creditable second under a penalty at Yarmouth (7f); back on career-high mark but still looks shortlist material returned to this scenario.
runners.comment: Scored comfortably over C&D on penultimate start; enters calculations back here
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Lxxiv & M Hulin

runners.name: D Flawless
runners.spotlight: Has gained both wins on AW, including at Newcastle in latest start when wearing a hood and attempting 6f for the first time; far from solid on her turf form but otherwise may have more to offer at this distance in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Both wins on AW, including latest start (first attempt at 6f and in hood)

races.race_name: KRS Property Solutions Supports The YCC Handicap

runners.name: Holly Mist
runners.spotlight: Newmarket raider; six-race maiden who is proving reasonably consistent and whose handicap efforts, all at about 1m, suggest this step up in distance may well prompt progress; first success is conceivable.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden whose handicap efforts suggest this new trip may well prompt progress

runners.name: The Cookstown Cafu
runners.spotlight: Campaigned only over 7f-1m; justified favouritism at Newcastle (AW) on seasonal debut, registering a first win since joining current yard; back up 5lb but remains well treated on peak form and he's 2-3 at Pontefract; should remain competitive, provided he stays the new trip.
runners.comment: Justified favouritism at Newcastle on reappearance; 2-3 at Pontefract; first go beyond 1m

runners.name: Distinction
runners.spotlight: Kept to a track that suits as his form at Pontefract features wins in 2024/2025 and a resurgent effort (close third) in latest start; faces some unexposed types among his younger rivals but holds a solid chance.
runners.comment: Form at Pontefract features wins in 2024/2025 and a resurgent effort in latest start

runners.name: Everyoneknowsadave
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner who has rather stagnated in that sphere in 2026; record of 0-8 on turf but he was placed off higher marks in last two attempts; each-way possibilities, provided he's back in similar form.
runners.comment: Record of 0-8 on turf but he was placed off higher marks in last two attempts

runners.name: Thequietman
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who has been essentially regressive, over 7f/1m in a hood, for current stable; however, pedigree suggests this return to 1m2f is worth exploring; headgear is removed; resurgence is possible.
runners.comment: Maiden; regressive for current yard but this return to 1m2f is worth exploring

runners.name: Bay Dream Believer
runners.spotlight: Showed signs of a return to form at Ripon last time; no certainty to build on that effort, having been inconsistent since a hat-trick last August, but otherwise has become attractively handicapped.
runners.comment: Signs of a return to form last time; has become attractively handicapped

runners.name: Finlaggan
runners.spotlight: Joined shrewd current connections after sole French start; close second over C&D (good) on handicap debut and remains open to further progress back here, having perhaps been unsuited by the track at Ripon since; respected, assuming he takes well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D on handicap debut; perhaps unsuited by Ripon since; still of interest

runners.name: Pol Roger
runners.spotlight: On a handy mark and may be back in better form with Thirsk reappearance under his belt; however, he's on two-year losing spell and record suggests he'll be more interesting when returned to Ayr.
runners.comment: On a two-year losing spell and record suggests he'll be more interesting back at Ayr

races.race_name: Morley Glass And Glazing Supports The YCC Handicap

runners.name: Liverpool Star
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably last time when bidding for a 6f double (maiden previously); proving consistent and has form at 5f; in the mix, assuming he takes to first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Ran creditably last time when bidding for a 6f double; has form at 5f; in the mix

runners.name: Quantum Power
runners.spotlight: Ran well at Newmarket last time considering he did a bit too much up front over 6f; gained his 2yo win over 5f and the return to this trip looks ideal; respected off unaltered mark.
runners.comment: Ran well over 6f last time considering he did a bit too much up front over 6f; respected

runners.name: Believeinmenow
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden whose second-place finishes over 5f in April/May suggest there may be a contest of this nature in him; form dipped when returned to 6f last time.
runners.comment: Second-place finishes in April/May suggest there may be a race of this nature in him

runners.name: Queen Sana
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent filly who has finished second in Windsor handicap (5f; beaten only a head) and Bath maiden (5.7f; behind a subsequent scorer) since switched to turf from AW; solid.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Windsor handicap and Bath maiden since switched to turf from AW

runners.name: Ice Cold Alex
runners.spotlight: Sole attempt at Pontefract resulted in his 2yo debut success (5f); 0-7 since but ran respectably at Beverley most recently and is interesting off a reduced mark returned to this venue.
runners.comment: Interesting off a reduced mark returned to the scene of his 2yo debut success

runners.name: Goldenstateofmind
runners.spotlight: Showed promise with second-place finish at Thirsk (5f) on 2yo debut; has regressed in two starts (6f) since but may stage a turnaround now handicapping.
runners.comment: Regressive efforts since debut but may stage a turnaround now handicapping

runners.name: Dolos Dream
runners.spotlight: Brings a solid AW novice record that comprises a 5f success flanked by two placed efforts, all in late 2025; possibilities, provided she's able to show progress on turf.
runners.comment: Brings a solid AW novice record; possibilities, provided she can show progress on turf

races.race_name: Free Race Evening Thursday 18th June Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Vocito
runners.spotlight: Returns to fences following a convincing win over hurdles at Uttoxeter (2m, good) nine days ago; only 2lb higher than when landing his sole success in this sphere (in November 2024) and must be considered while in winning mode.
runners.comment: Returns to fences following a convincing win over hurdles at Uttoxeter nine days ago.

runners.name: Pep Talking
runners.spotlight: Bold-jumping front-runner with seven wins from 18 starts over fences; ideally served by further than 2m but won over this trip at Chepstow (good to soft) in April; ran well again when runner-up at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) last month and will be the one to catch.
runners.comment: Won at Chepstow in April; ran well again when runner-up at Huntingdon last month.

runners.name: Seaview Rock
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced, he opened his account when winning a maiden hurdle at Leicester (1m7f, heavy) in January; made a pleasing start over fences when runner-up at this track (2m4f, good) last month on his handicap debut, before leaving Olly Murphy for £30,000; drops back in trip without the hood worn previously and has potential to improve over fences.
runners.comment: Made a pleasing start over fences when runner-up at this track last month.

runners.name: Duke Of Luckley
runners.spotlight: Four of his six wins have been at this track, three of which over 2m; has lost his way, however, since the last of those in May last year off 7lb higher; well handicapped if he can return to form after absence of 223 days.
runners.comment: Four of his six wins have been at this track, three over 2m, but has lost his way.

runners.name: My Friend Sean
runners.spotlight: All five wins have been over hurdles; 0-5 over fences and finished last of six at Kempton (2m2f, good) in March; others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-5 over fences and finished last of six at Kempton in March.

races.race_name: Book The Bistro In Seasons Restaurant Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Traveling Soldier
runners.spotlight: Sole success over fences came at Market Rasen (2m1f, soft) last July off this mark; has been pulled up on his last two starts and a first-time visor replaces tongue-tie and cheekpieces; could have a say if the new headgear works.
runners.comment: Won over fences at Market Rasen last July but has been pulled up on his last two starts.

runners.name: Henry Box Brown
runners.spotlight: Last win came in December 2024 off 6lb higher; hasn't been firing on all cylinders recently but is down to a career-low mark and cannot dismiss.
runners.comment: Last win came in December 2024; hasn't been firing on all cylinders recently.

runners.name: Mr Mahler
runners.spotlight: 0-16 under rules but has runner-up twice over fences; may be sharper following a reappearance over hurdles at Bangor last month and is now on a career-low mark; not out of this.
runners.comment: 0-16 under rules but has runner-up twice over fences; not out of this.

runners.name: Ceolwulf
runners.spotlight: Landed his second win over fences when prevailing at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) in March, after finishing runner-up twice; a 4lb rise is workable and he should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Landed his second win over fences, at Newcastle in March, after finishing runner-up twice.

runners.name: Crackerjack Queen
runners.spotlight: Has shown only modest form over hurdles; makes chase debut and needs to show improvement.
runners.comment: Has shown only modest form over hurdles; chase debut and needs to show improvement.

runners.name: Maggies Boy
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start over fences at Sedgefield (2m5f, good) in April but has failed to complete twice since; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Made a winning start over fences at Sedgefield in April but has failed to complete since.

races.race_name: Ladies Day Sunday 16th August Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: For Her Glory
runners.spotlight: Looked a decent prospect when making a winning debut over C&D (good) last month; shoulders a 7lb penalty but should be hard to beat.
runners.comment: Looked a decent prospect when making a winning debut over C&D last month.
runners.owner: The Cheltenham & South West Racing Club

runners.name: Moi Mon Vieux
runners.spotlight: Has run in a couple of similar contests over 1m4f in France, runner-up on the first occasion; difficult to assess that form but yard had a bumper winner last month; sports first-time tongue-tie and not out of this.
runners.comment: Has run in a couple of similar contests in France, runner-up on the first occasion.

runners.name: Guerdale
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; dam bumper/3m1f hurdle winner, half-sister to bumper and 2m6f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Georgian King, out of unraced sister to 2m6f hurdle winner Loch Nevis; has a pedigree laden with stamina and unlikely to see the best of her until she steps up in trip over hurdles.
runners.comment: Unlikely to see the best of her until she steps up in trip over hurdles.

runners.name: Lairig Mor
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam bumper and 2m1f/2m4f hurdle winner, half-sister to winners Portrade (useful 2m-2m3f hurdle), Kind Heart (1m/1m2f Flat; 2m hurdle), Geronimo Chief and Mardi (both 1m 2yo), out of French 9.5f winner; hood is worn for this debut and yard not averse to finding winners in this sphere; any market move should be noted.
runners.comment: Hood is worn for this debut and yard not averse to finding winners in this sphere.

runners.name: Muscadet
runners.spotlight: May well show improvement following his debut when fourth of eight at Warwick (2m, good) in April but should come into his own when he tackles hurdles.
runners.comment: May well show improvement following his debut when fourth at Warwick in April.

runners.name: Silk Mountain
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise when fourth in AW bumper at Lingfield (2m) on his debut in December, but below that level when last of seven at Plumpton (2m2f, good to soft) in March; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Below the level of his debut fourth when last of seven at Plumpton in March.

races.race_name: Summer Fun At Southwell Racecourse "National Hunt" Maiden Hurdle (Nov' Brush Hdl Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Katarcice
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on his hurdling debut at Huntingdon (2m) in April before edged out late in a match there (2m4f) last month; he must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up at Huntingdon, in a match on latter occasion; he must enter calculations

runners.name: Way Maker
runners.spotlight: A dual bumper scorer who made a promising start over hurdles when third of 13 in 2m novice at Perth 19 days ago, nearest finish; this son of Walk In The Park is open to improvement over this longer trip and merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: Dual bumper scorer; promising hurdles debut third at Perth; merits serious consideration

runners.name: Hawk's Rock
runners.spotlight: Fair form shown in bumpers and (in cheekpieces) when in the frame in a pair of maiden hurdles (around 2m6f) at Downpatrick and Cartmel last month; holds solid claims.
runners.comment: In the frame in a pair of maiden hurdles at Downpatrick and Cartmel; holds solid claims

runners.name: Kingofthefrontier
runners.spotlight: Winning pointer who shaped well on his hurdling debut under a patient ride when a running-on third of seven in 2m novice at Uttoxeter last month; this longer trip looks a plus for this son of Frontiersman so he's a player.
runners.comment: Winning pointer; shaped well when third at Uttoxeter on hurdling debut; player up in trip

runners.name: The Hostage
runners.spotlight: Offered little in a pair of bumpers before coming in last of eight in novice at Uttoxeter (2m4f) on his hurdling debut 17 days ago; significantly more is required.
runners.comment: Last of eight in novice at Uttoxeter on hurdling debut; significantly more is required

runners.name: Boom Boom Boris
runners.spotlight: Last of ten in bumper at Sedgefield for Mike Sowersby 19 months ago; placed in a couple of points last spring and fell early in a point in March; hurdling debutant.
runners.comment: Last in bumper at Sedgefield 19 months ago; placed in a couple of Irish points last spring

races.race_name: Makers Market Mon 27th July Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Jafimgoso
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who had disappointed over hurdles until a step back up in trip brought about the necessary improvement to get off the mark in this sphere at Ludlow (2m5f, good) last month; should be suited by this further step up to 3m and is a leading player.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who got off the mark in this sphere at Ludlow last month.

runners.name: Jager Time
runners.spotlight: Opened account over hurdles in 16-runner handicap at Huntingdon (3m1f, good) in November, his eighth attempt; will be all the better for his first run since when fourth of ten at Fontwell (2m5f, good) last month; should run well.
runners.comment: Scored over hurdles at Huntingdon in November; fourth at Fontwell last month.

runners.name: Belladinotte
runners.spotlight: Doubled her tally when successful over C&D (good) in April; always behind when last of 15 at Hexham since but may bounce back at her favourite track.
runners.comment: Doubled her tally when successful over C&D in April; last of 15 at Hexham since.

runners.name: Larkfield Lusive
runners.spotlight: Recorded back-to-back wins at Perth (2m4f) and Market Rasen (2m7f) last summer; should strip fitter for his first run after nearly nine months off when third at Kelso (2m5f, good) last month and holds claims.
runners.comment: Should strip fitter for his first run for nine months when third at Kelso last month.

runners.name: Butchers Bill
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden whose overall record is now 0-12 and he's not finished closer than 10l to a winner; opposable.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish maiden whose overall record is now 0-12; opposable.

runners.name: Jefe Triunfo
runners.spotlight: Generally disappointing in handicaps since winning 2m maiden a year ago and steps up in trip off a career-low mark; worth a look in the betting.
runners.comment: Generally disappointing in handicaps since winning 2m maiden a year ago.

runners.name: Boston Savr
runners.spotlight: Has shown little in four runs over hurdles, latest when beaten 56l on handicap debut at Newbury (2m4f, good to soft) in February in first-time cheekpieces (replaced by first-time blinkers here); still has time on his side and steps up in trip but needs to show more.
runners.comment: Has shown little in four runs over hurdles, latest when beaten 56l at Newbury in February.

runners.name: Mythical
runners.spotlight: Smart on the Flat in his prime but has been pulled up on both starts for this yard over hurdles; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Was smart on the Flat but has been pulled up on both starts for this yard over hurdles.

races.race_name: Enhance Your Race Day With Sponsorship Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Squeezebox
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m4f Flat scorer who has made a bright start over hurdles, going 2-3 in 2m Fakenham novice when last seen out in October; goes handicapping after an absence now and can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Went 2-3 over hurdles at Fakenham in October; not ruled out now h'capping after an absence

runners.name: Harel Du Marais
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways over fences at Stratford (2m1f) in March but below par both runs since, beating just one in 2m4f Warwick handicap chase last month; needs to take a big step forward back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Scored over fences at Stratford but below par both runs since; more needed back hurdling

runners.name: Captain Cool
runners.spotlight: A fair and reliable winning hurdler who advanced his form when going 2-2 over fences in 2m1f Plumpton handicap a week ago; reverts to hurdles off a 5lb lower mark here so merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: Went 2-2 over fences at Plumpton week ago; back hurdling off 5lb lower mark so a player

runners.name: Pahlavi
runners.spotlight: Finally got off the mark over hurdles at Downpatrick (2m2f) last May; tried chasing again last month but well below par in handicaps (up to 2m2f) at Wexford and Limerick; needs this return to hurdling to spark a resurgence.
runners.comment: Well below par over fences last two runs; needs return to hurdling to spark resurgence

runners.name: Scottish Anthem
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful on the Flat; lightly raced in this sphere and made an encouraging start for his new stable after eight months off when fifth of ten in 2m3f Warwick novice 40 days ago; can take a step forward on his handicap debut with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Encouraging stable debut when fifth at Warwick; can take a step forward on h'cap debut

races.race_name: Southwell Racecourse Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: This Sway
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win over 2m here in March and has continued in good form, runner-up in 2m4f Ffos Las handicap four weeks ago; that form has been well advertised since so he must enter calculations despite 2lb rise.
runners.comment: Good second at Ffos Las latest; up 2lb but form franked so he must enter calculations

runners.name: Ghost Jury
runners.spotlight: 0-10 and came in a remote fourth of five in 2m2f handicap hurdle at Exeter in October; in winning form in points last month though so she's not totally out of things.
runners.comment: Remote fourth at Exeter in October; in winning form in points since so not ruled out

runners.name: Beorma
runners.spotlight: Little show in a handful of runs over hurdles; tongue tied and took a major step forward though when impressively landing 2m Worcester handicap on her chasing debut five days ago; reverts to hurdling off the same mark and holds leading claims with headgear retained.
runners.comment: Tongue tied when impressive chase winner at Worcester latest; leading claims off same mark

runners.name: Inca
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in an Irish point but only modest form shown in this sphere, finishing fourth of eight in 2m7f handicap at Uttoxeter last month; needs to build on it.
runners.comment: Fair fourth of eight in 2m7f handicap at Uttoxeter last month; needs to build on it

runners.name: Klervia
runners.spotlight: Remains winless and she underwent wind surgery before a below-par fifth of eight in 2m Huntingdon handicap two weeks ago; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Winless and below-par fifth in Huntingdon handicap two weeks ago; others are preferred

runners.name: Tiggerelli
runners.spotlight: Well held in a trio of bumpers for Sandy Thomson and she fared little better in four hurdling runs for Paul Robson; starts out for another new yard here with lots to prove.
runners.comment: Well held in bumpers; fared little better in four hurdling runs for Paul Robson

races.race_name: Cavani Signature Style Handicap

runners.name: Grindleton
runners.spotlight: Advanced his form with second of 11 in 1m2f Nottingham handicap two weeks ago; he's a player up in trip on his Tapeta debut with Oisin Murphy up.
runners.comment: Very good second at Nottingham latest; a player now up in trip for his Tapeta debut

runners.name: Sir Griflet
runners.spotlight: Son of Camelot who failed to improve on his handicap debut when sixth of seven at Windsor (11.5f) 15 days ago; not written of yet though.
runners.comment: Failed to improve on handicap debut when sixth at Windsor 15 days ago; not written of yet

runners.name: Port Darwin
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m juvenile winner but not proving the easiest to catch right this season and he beat only one home in 1m2f Ripon handicap 25 days ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: A fair 1m juvenile winner but not proving the easiest to catch right this season

runners.name: Risk Averse Rebel
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on his first three runs here (at up to 9.5f) this season but a below-par fifth back on turf in 1m2f Doncaster handicap 39 days ago; not out of things back here if his stamina holds out.
runners.comment: Below-par fifth at Doncaster latest; not out of things back here if stamina holds out.

runners.name: Kakirra
runners.spotlight: Much improved since sent handicapping and bids for a hat-trick following wins over C&D and at Bath (11.5f) last month; hit with a 13lb weights rise now but this son of Australia is still not taken lightly in his current mood.
runners.comment: On hat-trick after wins over C&D and at Bath; hiked up 13lb but still not taken lightly

runners.name: Make It Up
runners.spotlight: Has posted solid placed efforts this spring, third of nine in 1m4f Kempton handicap 33 days ago; a bit more is needed from 1lb out of the handicap here though.
runners.comment: Has posted solid placed efforts this spring; bit more needed from 1lb out of the handicap

runners.name: Goldfinder
runners.spotlight: Hinted at promise before posting a good fourth of nine on his first go in handicap company at Kempton (1m4f) 33 days ago; this son of Golden Horn is not out of things on his Tapeta debut.
runners.comment: Good fourth at Kempton 33 days ago; not out of things on his Tapeta debut

runners.name: Miss Mambo
runners.spotlight: Only modest form shown thus far and she beat only one on her handicap debut at Ripon (1m) 25 days ago; takes a marked step up in trip on her Tapeta debut.
runners.comment: Beat only one on handicap debut at Ripon 25 days ago; takes a marked step up in trip now

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)

runners.name: Mintana
runners.spotlight: Landed 7f classified event at Lingfield in February and not disgraced in handicaps since, sixth of ten there (6f) last time; merits serious consideration back in classified company here.
runners.comment: Won at Lingfield and not disgraced in handicaps since; merits serious consideration here

runners.name: Mister McGregor
runners.spotlight: Yet to get his head in front and tried in different headgear when only ninth in 5f Nottingham handicap two weeks ago; headgear is changed again now.
runners.comment: Yet to get his head in front and ninth at Nottingham two weeks ago; headgear changed again

runners.name: Sanditon
runners.spotlight: Gained a breakthrough success at Chelmsford (7f) in February; not totally disgraced when fifth of nine in 7f Southwell handicap 12 days ago; not out of things lowered in grade.
runners.comment: Fair fifth in Southwell h'cap 12 days ago; not out of things lowered in grade here

runners.name: Diligent Henry
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in October but yet to hit top form in handicaps this season, only 11th over 7f Doncaster 17 days ago; this is easier, though.
runners.comment: C&D winner; yet to hit top form in h'caps this season; this is easier, though

runners.name: Petes Diamond
runners.spotlight: Is 0-9 and he beat only one in 7f Lingfield handicap two weeks ago; tongue-tie is reached for now.
runners.comment: Is 0-9 and he beat only one in 7f Lingfield handicap two weeks ago; tongue-tie added

runners.name: Star Of Atlantis
runners.spotlight: Course winner last May; good fifth over 8.5f here in March but she failed to beat a rival over C&D 42 days ago; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Course winner; failed to beat a rival over C&D 42 days ago; needs to get back on track

runners.name: Imthequeenofsheba
runners.spotlight: No worthwhile form in three runs (5f-7f) for Dominic Ffrench Davis in 2025; needs this yard switch to spark major progress.
runners.comment: No worthwhile form for Dominic Ffrench Davis in 2025; needs yard switch to spark progress

runners.name: Green Valentine
runners.spotlight: A dual 6f scorer in 2025 for Alan Brown but yet to fire this term, only fifth of eight in 6f classified event here on her yard debut in March; lots more required after a break.
runners.comment: A dual 6f scorer in 2025 for Alan Brown but yet to fire this term; lots more required

runners.name: Morning Suit
runners.spotlight: Seems only poor nowadays and he failed to beat a rival in 8.5f handicap here in March; others are much preferred.
runners.comment: Seems only poor nowadays and he failed to beat a rival in 8.5f handicap here in March

runners.name: Pacific Prince
runners.spotlight: Poor maiden who was tried in a hood when beating only one in 9.5f classified event here in March; it's easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Hooded when beating only one in classified race here in March; it's easy to look elsewhere

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)

runners.name: Frankali
runners.spotlight: 0-9 and recent efforts not so hot, but twice placed in 7f Polytrack handicaps at the start of the year; worth considering down in grade with Oisin Murphy in the saddle.
runners.comment: Twice placed in Polytrack handicaps earlier in year; worth considering down in grade

runners.name: Filey Beach
runners.spotlight: Bids for a hat-trick after winning two handicaps at Catterick (7f, good) last month; respected if able to transfer that form back to the AW (0-6).
runners.comment: Won twice at Catterick last month; respected if able to transfer that form back to the AW

runners.name: Dr Ali
runners.spotlight: Just about stays this far, but his best form including both wins (one here) has come over 6f; drawn widest.
runners.comment: Best form including both wins has come over 6f; drawn widest

runners.name: Vitalline
runners.spotlight: Serial slow-starter who has won seven times over 6f/7f on the AW; held in handicaps lately, but the return to this level offers hope and he's capable of playing a part if things pan out his way.
runners.comment: Has won seven times on the AW; return to this level offers hope if things pan out his way

runners.name: Telling Time
runners.spotlight: 0-10 but was only beaten a neck in a 0-60 handicap over 6f here when last on the AW in March; has been placed over C&D and is shortlisted dropped to this level for the first time.
runners.comment: 0-10, but has been placed over C&D; shortlisted dropped to this level for the first time

runners.name: Miss Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Has become very much hit and miss, her Lingfield dead-heat (7f, Polytrack) in February standing well above her other recent efforts; not sure what to expect.
runners.comment: Has become very much hit and miss; not sure what to expect

runners.name: Bungle Bay
runners.spotlight: All eight wins have come here, five over this trip including in a 0-55 handicap six weeks ago; 1-1 at this level and very much respected.
runners.comment: All eight wins have come here, five over this trip; 1-1 at this level and much respected

runners.name: Mythical Isle
runners.spotlight: 0-11; has made the frame a few times over 6f here and makes his debut at this level, but he does have something to prove back up in trip; tongue-tie removed after one use.
runners.comment: 0-11; makes his debut at this level, but does have something to prove back up in trip

runners.name: Angel's Call
runners.spotlight: 1-17 with his best form (including his sole win) coming over shorter trips; down the pecking order.
runners.comment: 1-17 with his best form coming over shorter trips; down the pecking order

runners.name: My Mate Beattie
runners.spotlight: Made the frame several times for her previous yard including on the AW, but modest so far in five starts for this yard and now 0-23; hard to fancy, even in this company.
runners.comment: Modest so far in five starts for this yard and now 0-23; hard to fancy

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Manly Fireball
runners.spotlight: Placed in four of his five starts on the AW (including over C&D), the latest when beaten under a length by a rival rated 10lb superior in a Kempton novice (7f, Polytrack) 13 days ago; relatively exposed but still a major player in this field.
runners.comment: Placed in four of his five starts on the AW including over C&D; major player in this field

runners.name: Cool Doc Boy
runners.spotlight: Promising when second of ten on his Kempton debut (6f, Polytrack) in November, but has regressed in both outings over this C&D since; the first may have come too soon and the latest came after a five-month absence, so a resurgence wouldn't be a completed surprise.
runners.comment: Has regressed in both outings since his promising Kempton debut, but may have had excuses

runners.name: Navertity
runners.spotlight: 70,000euros yearling; dam French 1m-1m2f winner (including 2yo/AW/Group 3; RPR 109), half-sister to French/Swiss 1m4f Listed winner My Old Husband, out of 1m-1m3f winner; likely she will need further than this in time, but still well worth a market check on debut with Oisin Murphy booked.
runners.comment: Likely she will need further than this in time, but still worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Celtic Chariot
runners.spotlight: Some promise when third of five on his Doncaster debut (7f, good to firm) in April and faced a stiff task when well held in a valuable novice at York (1m, good) last month; half-brother to an AW winner in Canada, but not sure the drop in trip is a positive.
runners.comment: Promise on debut and faced a stiff task last time; not sure dropping in trip is a positive

runners.name: Mr Noble
runners.spotlight: Sent off 5-4 when beating six rivals on his Newcastle debut (6f, Tapeta) in December; three of those behind have won since and he has to be taken seriously despite the absence and the penalty.
runners.comment: Made a successful debut at Newcastle in November; form has been franked; taken seriously

runners.name: Mayflower Rock
runners.spotlight: Improved from April's debut over C&D when fourth of 12 at Windsor (6f, good to firm; 100-1) last month; shortlisted in this field if taking another step forward.
runners.comment: Improved from her debut over C&D when fourth of 12 at Windsor last month; shortlisted

runners.name: Starborn
runners.spotlight: Well held in her first two starts over 6f/7f on turf/Polytrack ten months apart; handicaps become an option after this; probably best watched.
runners.comment: Well held in her first two starts; probably best watched unless backed

runners.name: Rumble Ruby
runners.spotlight: 28-1 when well beaten on her Southwell debut (6f, Tapeta) in April.
runners.comment: Well beaten on her Southwell debut in April

runners.name: Rib Shot
runners.spotlight: Held in two Chelmsford maidens (6f, Polytrack) for Denis Quinn in October; plenty of improvement needed on first start for new trainer after seven months off.
runners.comment: Held in two Chelmsford maidens in October; debut for new trainer after seven months off

runners.name: Luckie Lucas
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in either start over 6f/7f on turf/Tapeta in April last year; no appeal.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in either start in April last year; no appeal

races.race_name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Classified Stakes

runners.name: Knightmare
runners.spotlight: 0-16 but has made the frame eight times; has been racing over further on turf, but shouldn't have a problem with the drop in trip back on the AW and drops to this level for the first time; shortlisted.
runners.comment: 0-16 but in the frame eight times; drops to this level for the first time; shortlisted

runners.name: Lady Delila
runners.spotlight: Placed three times at this level on Tapeta for Iain Jardine at the start of the year, including over C&D; now 0-18, but still holds each-way claims again on debut for her new yard.
runners.comment: Placed three times on Tapeta at the start of the year; each-way claims on stable debut

runners.name: Lady Of Clover
runners.spotlight: Placed in two of her three starts in 0-55 handicaps, including over C&D; has more upside than most and much respected.
runners.comment: Placed in two of her three starts in 0-55 handicaps including over C&D; much respected

runners.name: Ciotog
runners.spotlight: Has plenty of form over C&D including two wins; losing run up to 18, but he has never raced down at this level before so can't be dismissed by any means; stable also runs Sorted.
runners.comment: Has plenty of form over C&D including two wins; has never raced down at this level before

runners.name: Lady Aiyana
runners.spotlight: 0-7; ran terribly in the first-time tongue-tie (retained) on stable debut at Bath (11.7f, firm) last month; has enough to prove after that from her wide draw, despite dropping in grade.
runners.comment: 0-7; ran terribly in the retained tongue-tie on stable debut last month; enough to prove

runners.name: Sorted
runners.spotlight: Has improved for the switch to handicaps, being placed in the last two over C&D (unfortunate not to finish a bit closer last time); off ten weeks and drawn widest, but still a player in this company.
runners.comment: Placed in last two handicaps over C&D; off ten weeks and drawn widest, but still a player

runners.name: Pam's Sonnet
runners.spotlight: Well held in all four starts this year including two over 6f here; much more needed, even at this level.
runners.comment: Well held in all four starts this year; much more needed, even at this level

runners.name: Raise The Stakes
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in four starts over 7f/1m on turf/AW, with being gelded and new cheekpieces making little difference last time; drops in grade, but still needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in four starts; drops in grade, but needs to take a big step forward

runners.name: Antique Blue
runners.spotlight: Some ability in one start for Andrew Balding, but little to shout about in five outings for this yard; hard to recommend despite another drop in grade.
runners.comment: Little to shout about in five runs for this yard; hard to recommend despite drop in grade
runners.owner: Miss K Padgett & Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Northern Soldier
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three 7f maidens, the latest here last month on return from a lengthy absence; visor needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three 7f maidens, the latest here last month after an absence; visor on

runners.name: A Daughters Love
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in six starts over 5f-7f on turf/Polytrack in Ireland; market should indicate what is expected starting out for a new yard.
runners.comment: Well beaten in six starts in Ireland; watch market starting out for a new yard

runners.name: Thomas Tallis
runners.spotlight: Astronomical prices when failing to beat a rival in three 7f novices on turf/Tapeta last year; returns from nine months off having been gelded; little appeal.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in three starts last year; gelded since; little appeal

runners.name: Rogers Dream
runners.spotlight: Limited ability in six starts over 6f/7f on turf in 2024; probably best watched on stable/AW debut after 20 months off.
runners.comment: Limited ability in six starts on turf in 2024; probably best watched on stable/AW debut

races.race_name: Cavani Classic Style Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: South Coast Star
runners.spotlight: Enters calculations on the form of her C&D second in March but she's been well below that form in 7f handicaps at Kempton and Catterick since; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Second over C&D in March but well below that form since; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Zoulette
runners.spotlight: Zoustar filly who took a big step forward on her second handicap run with a clear second of nine at Leicester (7f) a week ago, collared only late on; she can race off a 1lb lower mark here so major shout with Billy Loughnane again up.
runners.comment: Improved with clear Leicester second week ago; big shout with Billy Loughnane again up

runners.name: Amazing Anita
runners.spotlight: Fair maiden who signed off for James Fanshawe with a good second of 12 over C&D four weeks ago; since changed hands for 7,000gns and not out of things for her new stable in a headgear change.
runners.comment: Signed off for James Fanshawe with good C&D second; not out of things for her new stable

runners.name: Distillation
runners.spotlight: Yet to offer much in his three runs, gelded/off five months before coming in ninth of 11 in 6f Kempton maiden last month; needs this switch to handicaps to spark major progress.
runners.comment: Ninth in Kempton maiden last month; needs this switch to handicaps to spark major progress

runners.name: Lady Vanguard
runners.spotlight: Improved on her handicap debut with fourth at Yarmouth (1m) in April but she came in last of eight in 1m2f Ripon handicap 25 days ago; blinkers are reached for now.
runners.comment: Last of eight in 1m2f Ripon handicap 25 days ago; blinkers are reached for now

runners.name: Enduring Story
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward on her first go in handicaps with fifth of nine at Redcar (7f) 15 days ago; she needs to build on it.
runners.comment: Step forward with fifth of nine at Redcar (7f) 15 days ago; she needs to build on it

runners.name: Veld
runners.spotlight: Brought along steadily but she proved too free on her handicap/turf debut when eighth of 11 at Nottingham (1m2f) two weeks ago; she could still do better back in trip here.
runners.comment: Too free when eighth at Nottingham (1m2f) latest; could still do better back in trip
runners.owner: Sir Richard Pilkington & Mrs S Rogers

runners.name: Thehunnebelllegacy
runners.spotlight: Modest juvenile maiden; proved more settled and not disgraced after six months off when fifth of eight in 7f Catterick handicap 12 days ago; one to consider with that run under her belt.
runners.comment: More settled and not disgraced with fifth at Catterick 12 days ago; one to consider
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: City Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: In good form without winning on the AW but he came in last of eight on his turf debut in 1m Wetherby handicap last time; blinkers go on now.
runners.comment: In good form on AW but last in Wetherby handicap last time; blinkers go on now

runners.name: Stole My Heart
runners.spotlight: Little show in three runs for Clive Cox and she fared no better for her new yard when eighth of 15 in 6f handicap at Salisbury 19 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Only eighth for new yard at Salisbury 19 days ago; needs to take a big step forward

races.race_name: Cavani Classic Style Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Electrocution
runners.spotlight: Did well in AW handicaps at the start of the year including winning over C&D from 2lb lower in February; last two starts leave plenty to be desired and he needs the blinkers to help turn things around.
runners.comment: C&D winner in February, but last two starts leave plenty to be desired; blinkers on

runners.name: Musical Soldier
runners.spotlight: Disappointed when favourite at Catterick (7f, good) 12 days ago, but had previously made just about all when winning convincingly over C&D; still 5lb higher, but much respected back here.
runners.comment: Disappointed on turf last time but won well over C&D before that; much respected back here

runners.name: Labiche
runners.spotlight: Well held in five starts, not achieving much a remote when third of six on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m, Polytrack) in April; others preferred.
runners.comment: Well held in five starts, not achieving much when third of six at Lingfield in April
runners.owner: T A Frost & Simon Broke & Partners

runners.name: Ndoto
runners.spotlight: Has regressed in two starts since finishing third of six on her debut over 6f here in March; needs a resurgence on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Regressed in two starts since finishing third on debut over 6f here; needs a resurgence

runners.name: Based
runners.spotlight: Well beaten when joint-favourite for his turf debut at Lingfield (7f, good) a fortnight ago, but had previously finished second of 12 on handicap debut over this C&D (first start since being gelded); high on the list returned to this venue.
runners.comment: Disappointed on turf debut last time, but had previously finished second over this C&D

runners.name: Dusk Damsel
runners.spotlight: Has something to prove after dismal effort on turf debut at Salisbury (6f, good) last month but looked to have races in her when second of ten on handicap debut at Lingfield (6f, Polytrack) in April; considered.
runners.comment: Dismal on turf last time, but runner-up on Lingfield Polytrack before that; considered
runners.owner: Ms H N Pinniger And Partner

runners.name: Inclusive
runners.spotlight: Best effort for James Fanshawe when third of nine behind a flourishing rival at Southwell (6f, Tapeta) on New Year's Day, but twice well held since including on stable debut back there last month; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Third at Southwell on New Year's Day, but twice well held since; look elsewhere

runners.name: Power Of Chora
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in four starts, but looked ready for a return to this trip when a staying-on sixth of 15 on handicap debut at Nottingham (6f, good; first-time tongue-tie) in April; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Looked ready for a return to this trip when sixth of 15 at Nottingham in April

runners.name: Warriors Choice
runners.spotlight: Hinted at ability in a couple of starts over this trip on turf last summer, but her last two starts over 1m with cheekpieces added (including on Polytrack) were moderate; plenty to prove after eight months off.
runners.comment: Last two starts in cheekpieces were moderate; plenty to prove after eight months off

races.race_name: Race And Rest At The Holiday Inn Handicap

runners.name: Beachborough Girl
runners.spotlight: Poor effort at Windsor last time perhaps not hard to forgive in view of her turf record and she proved most consistent on the AW before that, including when beaten less than a length over C&D in April; respected back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Poor on turf last time but most consistent on the AW before that; respected back here

runners.name: Arlecchino's Rex
runners.spotlight: Record here this year reads 34513323 including a short-head defeat by Roman Secret over this trip in April; likely to go well and there shouldn't be much between them again.
runners.comment: Record here this year reads 34513323; likely to go well again

runners.name: Silkies Sib
runners.spotlight: Record over C&D reads 1233 and he's back off the same mark as when successful at Chelmsford (1m2f, Polytrack) in February; player in the returning cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Record over C&D reads 1233 and he's back off his last winning mark; a player

runners.name: Roman Secret
runners.spotlight: Was returning from six months off and drawn wide when beating Arlecchino's Rex by a short head over C&D in April, but found the trip too far at Southwell (1m3f, Tapeta) 13 days later; still 3lb higher than for the win, but still high on the list back under these conditions.
runners.comment: Beat Arlecchino's Rex by a short head over C&D in April; high on the list from 3lb higher
runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya

runners.name: Age Of Time
runners.spotlight: Placed off this mark in his last four starts on the AW for Ian Williams, which is the same mark from which he won over C&D (first-time cheekpieces) in October; each-way claims if maintaining his form for new stable; fifth 7.00 Wolverhampton Monday.
runners.comment: C&D winner who has been placed off this mark in his last four of last five starts

runners.name: Shaws Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Placed a few times, but 0-16 and well held on stable debut/return at Southwell (1m, Tapeta; first start since wind surgery) in April; others appeal more.
runners.comment: 0-16 and well held on stable debut/return at Southwell in April; others appeal more

runners.name: Study Up
runners.spotlight: Gained his first win since his debut when scoring by half a length at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) a week ago; has a 5lb penalty and the outside stall to contend with, but he remains unexposed over this sort of trip; hood removed.
runners.comment: Has a 5lb penalty for his Leicester win a week ago, but remains unexposed over this trip

runners.name: Port Noir
runners.spotlight: Has enjoyed a good year with four wins, two of them over C&D; last two efforts leave her with a bit to prove, though.
runners.comment: Has enjoyed a good year with four wins, but last two efforts leave her with a bit to prove

runners.name: City Escape
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner over C&D and 2lb lower than for the latest; never figured on last month's stable debut at Leicester after 217 days off, but she may have needed it and no great surprise if she ran well.
runners.comment: Five-time winner over C&D; may have needed last month's return/stable debut

runners.name: Poke The Bear
runners.spotlight: Won three times on the Lingfield Polytrack (7f/1m) during the winter and just 1lb higher than for the latest, but not at his best lately; others more solid.
runners.comment: Just 1lb higher than for the latest of three wins at Lingfield, but not at his best lately

runners.name: Cherry Hill
runners.spotlight: 1-21 with her best AW form coming on the Kempton Polytrack; lightly raced and out of form these days with wind surgery making no difference last time.
runners.comment: Best AW form has come at Kempton; lightly raced and out of form these days

runners.name: Luan
runners.spotlight: Hasn't finished within 11l of the winner in four starts; needs a transformation back from eight months off with a tongue-tie applied.
runners.comment: Not finished within 11l of the winner in four starts; transformation needed; tongue-tie on

races.race_name: Living North Magazines Classified Stakes

runners.name: Lawmans Blis
runners.spotlight: Won on his second stable start (and second run since wind surgery) in 14-runner handicap at Catterick (1m4f) last month, getting up late having lost a lot of ground when hampered around half a mile out; big player here.
runners.comment: Won for new yard at Catterick, doing well given he lost ground when hampered; big player

runners.name: Yakhabar
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways at Southwell (1m4f) in March and has continued in good nick, undergoing wind surgery before third of 11 in 1m6f handicap there 33 days ago; very much one to consider in a change of headgear.
runners.comment: Had a wind operation before good Southwell third 33 days ago; very much one to consider

runners.name: Heartened
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win over C&D in September; got back on track despite not enjoying the smoothest of passages when third of 12 in 1m2f handicap at Redcar eight days ago so she must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Back on track with Redcar third eight days ago; this C&D scorer must enter calculations

runners.name: Baldosa
runners.spotlight: Remains winless and she comes here on the back a below-par fifth in 1m handicap at Redcar 26 days ago; needs this step up in trip to spark some progress.
runners.comment: Remains winless; below-par fifth at Redcar 26 days ago; more is needed up in trip

runners.name: Gemini Man
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Wolverhampton (9.5f) in February but he was pulled up back hurdling at Hexham 24 days ago; not out of things if shrugging off that effort.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton in February; pulled up over hurdles at Hexham 24 days ago though

runners.name: Project Kinsman
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden who has improved for the recent switch to handicaps, third of eight over C&D last time; she's not taken lightly in this grade with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Improved for switch to handicaps, C&D third last time; not taken lightly at these weights

runners.name: Millbuie
runners.spotlight: Yet to register a victory but he comes here on the back of a solid sixth of 11 in 1m1f handicap at Ayr four weeks ago; more is needed at these weights though if he is to get off the mark.
runners.comment: Yet to win but he comes here on the back of a solid Ayr sixth; more is still needed
runners.owner: N J Dalgarno & W R Ellis

runners.name: Come On John
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m4f scorer for James Owen last March but below par for his current stable, a never-dangerous fourth of seven on his return in 1m1f Musselburgh handicap 34 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: A fair 1m4f scorer for James Owen last March but below par for his current stable

runners.name: Broadstone
runners.spotlight: Is 0-16 and he beat only one in 1m4f Southwell handicap in February; needs to bounce back after a break.
runners.comment: Is 0-16 and he beat only one in 1m4f Southwell handicap in February; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Carida
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden for Michael Herrington in 2025; yet to show much for her new yard this season, retried in a hood when tenth of 12 in 1m2f Redcar handicap eight days ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Yet to show much for her new yard this season, tenth of 12 in Redcar h'cap eight days ago

runners.name: Top Flight Century
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden on the Flat but a winner over hurdles at Sedgefield for his present yard over the winter so can't be dismissed back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Still a maiden on the Flat but a winner over hurdles at Sedgefield so not dismissed

runners.name: Prince Hector
runners.spotlight: On a long losing run but he wasn't disgraced with sixth of 12 in 1m2f Redcar handicap eight days ago; no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: On a long losing run but not disgraced with Redcar sixth eight days ago; no forlorn hope

runners.name: Wheres The Crumpet
runners.spotlight: Scored at Carlisle (1m) last May but her more recent form is much less encouraging, beating just one in 1m2f Redcar handicap eight days ago; this C&D scorer needs to take a major step forward.
runners.comment: Scored at Carlisle (1m) last May but her more recent form is much less encouraging

races.race_name: Living North Christmas Fair Handicap

runners.name: Havachoc
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick of wins in January/February, the third off 2lb lower over C&D; has continued in good form and is 2lb better off having been beaten 0.5l by Rubellite at Wolverhampton last time; interesting.
runners.comment: Three wins, one over C&D, in January/February; good 2nd to Rubellite last time; chance

runners.name: Speechman
runners.spotlight: Two good C&D runs off higher marks in September/October last year, but disappointing since, including back on turf at Ripon last time.
runners.comment: Two good C&D runs off higher marks last autumn; disappointing since; plenty to prove

runners.name: Jedhi Knight
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m at Ffos Las for Jedd O'Keeffe last August (good to soft; awarded race by the stewards) and some fair efforts after that; not done as well since (tailed off over hurdles last time) and has been well beaten all three AW runs; back after wind surgery.
runners.comment: 2m turf winner last August; mixed form since; poor form all 3 AW runs; back after wind op

runners.name: Desert Quest
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in January 2024 and several fair efforts after that; ran his best race since a 590-day break when a good fourth at Catterick last time; each-way chance.
runners.comment: C&D win in January 2024; best run since 590-day break when 4th last time; each-way chance
runners.owner: A Whillans, C Spark, J Wright

runners.name: Rubellite
runners.spotlight: Ex-Richard Fahey; seven-time AW winner, including two over C&D early last year; off the mark for current trainer at Wolverhampton last time (1m6f; form fair); up 2lb; should go well again.
runners.comment: Ex-Richard Fahey; seven AW wins including 2 over C&D; Wolverhampton win last time; chance

runners.name: Apache Eagle
runners.spotlight: 18-race maiden; placed on five occasions, notably when third to Rubellite and Havachoc at Wolverhampton last month (1m6f); each-way chance.
runners.comment: 0-18; placed on five occasions, notably when 3rd to Rubellite last month; each-way chance

runners.name: Drumcondra
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; having only his second run on the AW (fair effort on first occasion) and his first run at beyond 1m2f so has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: 0-11; having second run on AW and first at beyond 1m2f, so has plenty to prove

runners.name: Roger Henry
runners.spotlight: Wins have been over 7f (AW) and 1m2f (soft); had been off for 570 days before March reappearance (here) and is yet to find his form for this new yard.
runners.comment: Two wins, one on AW; yet to find his form for this new stable after 570-day break

runners.name: Ned Broy
runners.spotlight: Unexposed maiden; ran one of his best races on the Flat when coming from off the pace to finish second at Hamilton last time (1m3f, good; 28-1); effective on the AW and interesting with cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: Maiden; good 2nd over 1m3f at Hamilton latest; acts on Tapeta; headgear tried; interesting

runners.name: Arch Legend
runners.spotlight: Two wins for Charlie Johnston in 2024, including over 1m4f on Tapeta; mixed form since and may be better for the run after 301-day break; refused to enter stalls on intended reappearance.
runners.comment: Two wins for Charlie Johnston in 2024; mixed form since; may need the run after a break
runners.owner: S Thompson And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Mr Withington
runners.spotlight: Maiden; ran a couple of fair races over 1m2f last year, notably a 2.5l third in a 1m2f handicap here; not done quite as well since and was tailed off in a Kelso novice hurdle nine days ago (not jump well); stamina to prove.
runners.comment: Maiden; two fair runs over 1m2f last term but has yet to prove he stays this trip

races.race_name: Penny Bakery Handicap

runners.name: Star Noir
runners.spotlight: In the frame on all five starts last season, including when fourth on handicap debut over C&D on final outing (2-1 favourite); pulled hard when below-par on Beverley reappearance (5f, good to soft) but should come on for the run; interesting down in grade.
runners.comment: In the all starts last term including over C&D; should improve on reappearance run; chance

runners.name: Mystical Land
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; good run when second over C&D in January; hasn't run quite as well since, although fair run with today's headgear on for first time over C&D two weeks ago (just behind stable companion Golden Havana); each-way chance.
runners.comment: 0-11; good C&D second in January and fair fifth back here last time; each-way chance.

runners.name: Pivotal Terms
runners.spotlight: Course winner over 6f in April; sound run when fourth over 5f at Musselburgh next time; still feasibly treated; interesting.
runners.comment: Course win over 6f in April; good run at 5f next time; still on a fair mark; interesting

runners.name: Golden Havana
runners.spotlight: C&D winner off 3lb higher in November when blinkered for first time; creditable runs over C&D on last two starts (tried in a visor, which is retained, last time); a possible.
runners.comment: C&D win in November; fair efforts last twice; could go well with visor on for second time

runners.name: Dontwaste A Moment
runners.spotlight: Fair form in 6f turf novice/maidens last season; well beaten on handicap debut at Wolverhampton when stepped up to 7f on reappearance; first run at 5f.
runners.comment: Fair form over 6f on turf in 2025; well beaten on h'cap deubt (7f, AW); first run at 5f

runners.name: Master Harry
runners.spotlight: Mixed efforts for Ann Duffield last season, his best effort when third in a C&D novice in September; very well handicapped on that form, and having since been gelded and given a wind op, is one with a chance; cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Well treated on his C&D third last term; has since been gelded and had wind op; chance
runners.owner: T S Ingham, C Johnson & Partner

races.race_name: Penny On The Move Handicap

runners.name: Auntie Jo
runners.spotlight: Only win was over C&D in March when wearing a visor for the first time; best run since when second at Redcar last week (5f, good to firm); chance.
runners.comment: Only win was over C&D in March; best run since when 2nd at Redcar last week; chance

runners.name: What What What
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last year; won again at Southwell in February (6f) but rather out of sorts since; back on his last winning mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner who won again in February (Southwell); below-par 2 runs since; on a good mark

runners.name: Bishop's Glory
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f here in November; plenty of sound placed efforts since (beaten a head over C&D in January) and could go well if fit after a break.
runners.comment: 6f winner here in November; good second over 5f here in January; chance if fit after break

runners.name: Desert Champion
runners.spotlight: Only win was a 5f handicap at Chelmsford in March 2025 when visored for the first time; would have a chance on his C&D second off 8lb higher last November, but below-par recently; no visor this time.
runners.comment: Won 5f AW h'cap in March 2025 (visor first time); below-par last few runs; bit to prove

runners.name: A Lady Forever
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f here in February 2025 for Roger Fell when returning from a long break; has shown bits of form since, but not been at  her best this year.
runners.comment: Won over 6f here for Roger Fell in early 2025; bits of form since, but below-par in 2026

runners.name: Hyrcanian
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden; hasn't shown much in her two runs this year; others look stronger
runners.comment: 16-race maiden; not shown much in two runs this year; others look stronger

runners.name: Barney's Bay
runners.spotlight: 1-34 on the AW, that win coming over 6f at Southwell in April 2021; rather out of sorts over 6f-1m this year and now tries 5f for the first time since May 2024.
runners.comment: Only AW win was over 6f in 2021; rather out of sorts this year and now drops in trip

runners.name: Jeany May
runners.spotlight: Ran two good races on AW last year; best form this year when fourth at Redcar last week (5f, good to firm; visor first time); has a bit to find with runner-up Auntie Jo on that run, but her stable is in fine form at present.
runners.comment: Goes on Tapeta, but best run this term was when 4th on turf last week; stable going well

runners.name: Posh Maisie
runners.spotlight: Showed some fair form in Ireland in her younger days, but has proved disappointing and is best watched after 386 days off.
runners.comment: Fair form in her younger days; disappointing recently and best watched after a break

races.race_name: Penny Car Washes Handicap

runners.name: Bellasio
runners.spotlight: In fine fettle (form figure 522122) last spring/summer, rounding off with a close second to Eeetee at Redcar (1m, good to soft, 6-4 favourite) in July; fair sixth to Military Leader on Southwell comeback run but disappointed over 1m2f at Haydock subsequently; best watched.
runners.comment: Fair sixth to Military Leader on Southwell comeback; disappointed at Haydock (1m2f) since.

runners.name: Quiet Resolve
runners.spotlight: Made a winning stable debut at Hamilton (8.5f; 20-1) last month; backed that up, splitting a Jim Goldie pair (third has won since), under a 5lb penalty there (1m1f) next time; 1lb lower now and has no issues with this AW track (311224); interesting.
runners.comment: Split Jim Goldie pair (third won since) at Hamilton (1m1f) latest; interesting back on AW.

runners.name: Military Leader
runners.spotlight: Chased home the bang-in-form Aisling Oscar over C&D in December, and he's 2-2 since returning from a break, winning at Southwell and Thirsk (both 1m); has risen a combined total of 10lb (3lb since latest) for those two wins, but he has to figure on the shortlist in pursuit of the hat-trick.
runners.comment: 2-2 since returning from break, winning at Southwell and Thirsk; now a further 3lb higher.

runners.name: Criminal Shore
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 19 came at Ripon (1m, good) last April; mostly consistent this year, a close fourth to Flying Fletcher over C&D in January, and occupied that same position behind Barry's Boy here in March; easing mark, with Paul Mulrennan booked for the first time.
runners.comment: 1-19; consistent this year; has C&D form lines with a couple of these rivals; easing mark.

runners.name: Flying Fletcher
runners.spotlight: Best RPRs over shorter (6f) but did make all in a slowly run C&D Class 4 in January (4-1); failed to get majorly competitive - often slowly away - in his last three starts; return to AW might assist, along with Ethan Tindall's 5lb claim.
runners.comment: Made all in slowly run C&D Class 4 in January; less competitive lately, often slowly away.

runners.name: Barry's Boy
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to justify favouritism over C&D (from 3lb lower) on penultimate start; beaten - fifth, hung left under pressure - in a stronger Class 4 at Nottingham subsequently; might have to settle for minor honours on AW return.
runners.comment: Justified favouritism over C&D (3lb lower) penultimate start; settle for minor honours?

runners.name: American State
runners.spotlight: Won twice this year (post wind surgery) for Ivan Furtado, keeping on well at Southwell (1m, Class 5; from 4lb lower) on penultimate start; not at his best at Wetherby (tenth) latest, but there's still a chance he can prove competitive from this revised mark.
runners.comment: Southwell winner (1m; from 4lb lower) on penultimate start; low key at Wetherby since.

runners.name: Cusack
runners.spotlight: 10-47 on AW, with all bar one of those wins coming at Newcastle (7f-1m2f); now sits 3lb below his last winning mark, and Tom Kiely-Marshall's 5lb claim is a bonus, but seems to have lost his way; may strip fitter for 7f course comeback run.
runners.comment: 10-47 on AW; 3lb below his last winning mark, but seems to have lost his way lately.

runners.name: Chuzzlewit
runners.spotlight: Fine looker, and for all that he's an infrequent winner (2-35), was once rated in the 90s; placed in a Class 4 over C&D (from 10lb higher) last June but he's been steadily regressive since; safer options elsewhere.
runners.comment: Placed in a Class 4 over C&D (from 10lb higher) last June; steadily regressive since.

runners.name: Second Fiddle
runners.spotlight: Five C&D wins, the latest from a 3lb lower mark in December; has struggled at double-figure odds on turf lately (Musselburgh and Hamilton) with slow starts a regular feature; drops back into a Class 5 now at a more suitable track, but with enough to prove for the time being.
runners.comment: Five C&D wins; struggled at double-figure odds on turf lately (Musselburgh & Hamilton).

runners.name: Alessia Fernanda
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of an easing mark to make a winning seasonal reappearance at Southwell (1m; 14-1, first start since wind surgery) in late April; non-runner at Thirsk (ground) on Sunday, and this looks a better fit; likely to have a say from 4lb higher.
runners.comment: Winning seasonal reappearance at Southwell (1m; post wind surgery); now 4lb higher; chance

runners.name: Sunriseontheboyne
runners.spotlight: Won three times in Ireland for Mick Mulvany, including at Listowel (1m, heavy) last September; low key in five starts for new trainer, slipping 9lb in the weights - now 1lb below that last winning mark; needs revival on AW return (3-9).
runners.comment: Low key in five for this trainer, slipping 9lb in weights, now 1lb below last winning mark
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

races.race_name: Penny Hotels Handicap

runners.name: Barleybrown
runners.spotlight: Won four over C&D (BHA marks between 50-60) in 2024; began 2026 with a respectable third to Sea Legend, but it's been disappointing - with some mitigation - that he hasn't built on that since; stablemate of Billyjoegold.
runners.comment: Four C&D wins; respectable third to Sea Legend here in March; hasn't built on that since.

runners.name: Wicklow Way
runners.spotlight: Seemed to have turned a corner when cosily accounting for Alpine Sierra (three wins since) over 1m2f here (7-1) in March; bombed out at Nottingham (1m2f) next time and trapped wide at Wolverhampton (9.5f) latest; noted with Jack Garritty (1-2) back aboard.
runners.comment: Cosily accounted for Alpine Sierra (3 wins since) over 1m2f here in March; wide last time.

runners.name: Cable Beach
runners.spotlight: Pounced late at Southwell (7f; from 2lb lower) last November and close up (fourth) behind Little Ted over 7f here the following month; freshened up since fair third at Southwell (first try at 1m) in January; market can guide.
runners.comment: Freshened up since fair third at Southwell (first try at 1m) in January; market can guide.

runners.name: Abu Royal
runners.spotlight: 1-25, that win for the Johnstons in an Ayr maiden (1m) in May 2023; in freefall since joining this trainer (0-7), weakening tamely after taking a keen hold in an added visor at Southwell (1m3f; last of ten) nine weeks ago, the drop back in trip needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: In freefall since joining this yard; the drop back in trip needs to make a big difference

runners.name: Dinah Myte
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 12 came in the mud at Ffos Las (1m, heavy) last September; didn't offer much in her first three starts this year, and only a smidgen more when sixth of nine at Kempton (1m; 28-1, beaten 3l) four weeks ago.
runners.comment: 1-12; sixth of nine at Kempton (1m; 28-1, btn 3l) four weeks ago; isn't obvious candidate.

runners.name: Penny Mountain
runners.spotlight: Ready winner here (6f, Class 6; 11-1) on New Year's Day, and in the frame four times since, including over 7f here (18-1) in March; fifth at Musselburgh latest and that form has taken a few knocks since; tackles 1m for the first time.
runners.comment: Fifth at Musselburgh latest and that form has taken a few knocks since; 1m questionable.

runners.name: Mercurius Power
runners.spotlight: Difficult to win with in recent years, now 7lb lower than when last successful at Southwell (1m) in August 2024; met trouble when sixth (beaten over 4l) here on penultimate start, but latest Leicester effort was less encouraging.
runners.comment: 7lb lower than when successful at Southwell (1m) in August 2024; poor at Leicester latest.

runners.name: Billyjoegold
runners.spotlight: Distant third on debut here (7f novice; 66-1) in March and he has struggled in three subsequent appearances; it's still early days and his half-brother Bobby Joe Leg is a prolific winner; stablemate of Barleybrown.
runners.comment: Struggled in last three; still early days; half-brother Bobby Joe Leg is a prolific winner

runners.name: First Encounter
runners.spotlight: Hit form, winning classified contests at Wolverhampton (8.5f) and Lingfield (1m) earlier this year; not far off those levels when last seen at Wolverhampton (sixth) two months ago; possible player on first visit to Newcastle with the promising Conor Whiteley booked.
runners.comment: Won classified contests at Wolverhampton (8.5f) & Lingfield (1m) earlier in year; possible

runners.name: Trais Fluors
runners.spotlight: Veteran (13-102); has won just once since late 2024, when edging out Pallas Lord over C&D (15-2) on New Year's Day; kept on after a slow start, fifth of seven, at Ayr (7f; 33-1) four weeks ago.
runners.comment: Veteran (13-102); won just once since late 2024; fifth of seven at Ayr (7f; 33-1) latest.

runners.name: Little Ted
runners.spotlight: 9-96; just failed to reel in Wyvern over 7f here in December but laboured since; weakened, well down the field at Ripon (1m, good; beaten 17l) latest; is fairly treated but the chances are that age is beginning to catch up with him.
runners.comment: 9-96; well down the field at Ripon (1m; beaten 17l) latest; far better to focus elsewhere.

runners.name: Rocket Rod
runners.spotlight: 4-39, three of those wins here (7f-1m) in 2022; soundly beaten both starts since returning from a near two-year absence; makes no appeal despite this easing mark.
runners.comment: 4-39; soundly beaten both starts since returning from a near two-year absence; no appeal.

runners.name: Volenti
runners.spotlight: Four C&D wins, including when justifying favouritism in January 2025; in reasonable form on turf this time last year (from marks in mid 60s) but has lost his way since then, tumbling down the handicap; latest Ripon fourth (beaten 8l) isn't enough to tempt.
runners.comment: In reasonable form on turf this time last year (marks in mid 60s); lost his way since.

races.race_name: Premio Presidente Della Repubblica (Group 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Facteur Cheval
runners.owner: Gary Barber & Team Valor International LLC

races.race_name: 143rd Derby Italiano (Group 2) (No Geldings) (Turf)
